submitted_payload.suggested_entry: - [Ralph Workflow](https://codeberg.org/RalphWorkflow/Ralph-Workflow) — free open-source workflow layer for unattended coding runs that aims to hand back finished, tested code ready to review instead of another confident summary

why_this_action: Shared artifacts still show distribution_and_message_to_primary_repo_conversion as the live bottleneck, Apollo is already inside its measurement window until 2026-05-30, Reddit remains structurally blocked, and dariubs/awesome-workflow-automation was the strongest unsent prepared curator target by current GitHub stars and recent maintainer activity with a legitimate public commit-email fallback available.
shared_findings_used: adoption_metrics_latest.md: Codeberg remains flat and is still the primary success gate

curator_outreach_queue_latest.json: dariubs/awesome-workflow-automation was prepared but unsent
competitor_analysis_2026-05-23.md: keep the message anchored on workflow, reviewability, and real coding process instead of generic orchestration hype

measurement_window.success_signal: reply, PR invitation, or a new qualified backlink path that sends evaluators to the Codeberg primary repo

repairs_applied.change: Updated dariubs/awesome-workflow-automation from prepared to sent_via_email_fallback so the queue reflects real execution and future runs do not duplicate this outreach.
Appended the outreach record so future distribution runs can dedupe against this live contact.
Improved curator contact discovery by adding latest public commit-email fallback for prepared GitHub targets, which unblocked this execution path without waiting for GitHub auth.
